summary: Closed PR proposing two gmregclear function decompilations, fn_8017DE54 and fn_8017F14C, with matching report evidence for both. It also updated gmregclear.h prototypes and split a lbl_80472D28 struct padding range to expose x10A. The PR was later described by the author as consolidated into other PRs; no merge is recorded in the slice.

key_files.role: Added C bodies for fn_8017DE54 and fn_8017F14C; exposed struct lbl_80472D28_t field x10A at offset 0x10A.
Replaced UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototypes for fn_8017DE54 and fn_8017F14C with recovered signatures.

what_changed.text: fn_8017DE54 was decompiled as s32 fn_8017DE54(u8, u8*) with flag checks on 0x20, 0x10, and 2, plus three byte comparisons against 0x21.
fn_8017F14C was decompiled as s32 fn_8017F14C(void* arg0), gated by gm_8016AE38()->x24C8.x4_7, reading a s32 at arg0+0x98, clamping values above 9999, and multiplying by lbl_80472D28.x10A.
struct lbl_80472D28_t padding from +0x108 was split to add s16 x10A at +0x10A.
gmregclear.h prototypes for fn_8017DE54 and fn_8017F14C were updated from unknown signatures to typed declarations.
The PR body claimed ninja built clean and fuzzy_match_percent was 100.0 for all functions; the bot report listed two new 100.00% matches totaling +224 bytes.

smart_moves.text: Updated the owning header prototypes alongside the recovered function bodies instead of leaving UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
Kept fn_ address-style global names rather than inventing semantic names for functions whose meaning was not established by the slice.

Documented why a raw arg0+0x98 access remained in fn_8017F14C: the author stated the parameter was void*, no known decompiled callers identified the real struct type, and the offset landed in unnamed padding of the closest candidate struct.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When decompiling a function body proves a signature, update the owning header at the same time rather than retaining UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.

Raw pointer math should be treated as suspect and documented when it cannot yet be replaced by a typed field; in this slice, fn_8017F14C kept arg0+0x98 because the argument type and callers were not known according to the author comment.

naming_conventions.text: Address-style names fn_8017DE54 and fn_8017F14C were retained; the slice does not provide semantic evidence for renaming them.
The newly exposed struct member used offset-style name x10A, consistent with limited semantic evidence for lbl_80472D28_t.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: fn_8017DE54 used explicit s32 casts for byte comparisons against 0x21 and a separate pointer p = &arg1[1], likely preserving the required code shape for the claimed match.
fn_8017F14C used a local data pointer to lbl_80472D28, retained a raw s32 load from arg0+0x98, and used a separate ret local after capping val above 9999.
Verification evidence in the slice is limited to the PR body's ninja claim and bot fuzzy-match report; no objdiff log or reviewer validation is included.

review_feedback.text: No review comments are present in the slice.

The author first said pointer math would be fixed soon, then later stated the fn_8017F14C arg0+0x98 pointer math could not be replaced with struct field access because the true type and callers were unknown.
The closing comment says the work was consolidated into other PRs.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that consolidated or replaced PR #2264 gmregclear fn_8017DE54 fn_8017F14C
Search current gmregclear.c for fn_8017F14C arg0 + 0x98 pointer math
Search current gmregclear.h for fn_8017DE54 and fn_8017F14C final prototypes
Search for callers of fn_8017F14C to recover the void* argument type
Search lbl_80472D28_t x10A current field naming and offset ownership

notes: Strong evidence for changed files and exact diff content.
Match evidence is present from the PR body and bot fuzzy report, but no objdiff/checkdiff artifact is included.
No review comments are present; pointer-math discussion is from author issue comments, one of which is explicitly signed '--Claude'.
The PR is closed with merged_at null and an author comment says it was consolidated into other PRs, so this intake does not treat the changes as accepted or merged.
The claim that fn_8017F14C has no known decompiled callers and no known concrete argument type is only evidenced by an issue comment, not independently verified in this intake.

accepted_candidate_records.title: gmregclear header prototypes updated with matched bodies
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2264 changed fn_8017DE54 and fn_8017F14C from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations to typed gmregclear.h prototypes in the same diff that added their C bodies.

source_update_candidates.title: PR #2264 pointer-math note for fn_8017F14C
source_update_candidates.text: In PR #2264, fn_8017F14C retained `*(s32*) ((u8*) arg0 + 0x98)`; an author comment stated this could not be replaced with struct field access because arg0 was void*, no known decompiled callers identified the actual struct type, and offset 0x98 fell within unnamed padding in the closest candidate struct.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential path fact for future gmregclear type recovery, but should be curated cautiously because the PR was not merged and the explanation was not independently verified in the slice.

source_update_candidates.title: PR #2264 exposed lbl_80472D28_t x10A
source_update_candidates.text: The PR split lbl_80472D28_t padding to add `s16 x10A` at offset +0x10A, used by fn_8017F14C as a multiplier.
source_update_candidates.reason: May help future struct recovery, but acceptance depends on later consolidated PRs or current source confirmation.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the raw arg0+0x98 access as preferred style; the loaded global standard still prefers typed fields when recoverable.
Do not infer semantic names for fn_8017DE54, fn_8017F14C, or x10A from this slice.
Do not mark the PR as merged or accepted; metadata shows merged_at null and the closing comment says it was consolidated into other PRs.
